title: Open Shortest Path First
description: Link-state IGP that uses Dijkstra's algorithm to find shortest paths within an autonomous system.
fullExplanation: OSPF routers form adjacencies by exchanging Hello packets. They flood LSAs (Link State Advertisements) to build an identical LSDB (Link State Database) across all routers in an area. Each router independently runs Dijkstra's SPF algorithm to compute the shortest path tree. Area 0 is the backbone — all other areas must connect to it. DR/BDR election on multi-access networks reduces LSA flooding.

packetTypes.name: Hello
packetTypes.description: Discover and maintain neighbors. Sent every 10s (30s on NBMA).

packetTypes.name: DBD
packetTypes.description: Database Description — summary of LSDB for initial sync.

packetTypes.name: LSR
packetTypes.description: Link State Request — request specific LSAs.

packetTypes.name: LSU
packetTypes.description: Link State Update — contains actual LSAs.

packetTypes.name: LSAck
packetTypes.description: Link State Acknowledgement — confirms LSU receipt.

lsaTypes.name: Router LSA
lsaTypes.description: Each router's links and costs. Flooded within area.

keyFacts: DR/BDR elected by highest priority (default 1), then highest Router ID
OSPFv2: IPv4 only. OSPFv3: IPv6 (and IPv4 with address families)
OSPF areas reduce LSA flooding and SPF computation
Stub area: no external LSAs. Totally stubby: no external or inter-area LSAs
